name: HillaryClinton.com
subject: An update on this race:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2
body:
Here's where we stand: | Derek --

I want to give you an update on the state of the race, but first, I want to say thank you. If you do enough campaigns, you know that August can be a month where it's tough to break through -- and there were certainly times over the past few weeks when we had to ask you for some extra help.

But here's the thing: Every time we reached out and explained what was going on, you stepped up.

So we came together and raised more than $140 million this past month. Even better for the road ahead, more than 2.3 million people have now donated to HFA -- and the average gift to the campaign in August was about $50.

We’re building an incredible campaign from the ground up. We’ve opened offices all across the country, our volunteer capacity is growing every day, and we’re well on our way to hitting our goal of getting 3 million people registered or committed to vote.

Knowing all that, I feel good about where we are in terms of our organization, our resources, and our enthusiasm. You should feel good, too.

But now we’re in September, Derek. The entire country is going to be paying attention to what we’re doing, how hard we’re fighting, and whether we have what it takes to defeat Donald Trump.

We're facing a once-in-a-generation opponent -- someone who almost gleefully rejects the fundamental values that define who we are as a people and eagerly ignores the norms of how we've practiced our democracy for decades. But we've known that.

Now he's also given us everything we need to predict his strategy going forward.

He shook up his campaign (again) and brought on the head of _Brietbart News_ , a platform of the alt-right that traffics in racist, sexist, and anti-immigrant sentiments, to run the show. He's added a new line to his stump speech -- yelling (to almost-all-white audiences) that African Americans should support him because they “have nothing to lose.” Yesterday, he took a spur-of-the-moment trip to Mexico, met with the country’s president, then lied to the American people about whether or not the two men discussed who would pay for that wall Trump wants to build (they did -- Mexico said it won't be paying).

If we let ourselves get complacent about what a danger Trump represents, or allow the constant barrage of offensive moments from Trump to somehow become normal, then we're creating a scenario in which he will win this election.

We're seeing state poll after state poll where our two campaigns are neck-and-neck. We haven’t heard what his August fundraising numbers were, but we suspect they’re going to be very strong. And he’s reserving a lot more TV time for advertising, so we need to prepare ourselves for the impact of that. Trump is throwing his entire legacy (and multiple million-dollar personal checks) into this campaign.

So we’re going to fight hard and proud.

We’re going to take everything we’ve built over the last 18 months -- the organizers we’ve hired, the offices we’ve opened, the skills we’ve honed -- and we’re going to put it to work.

In the two months ahead, we’ve got events, rallies, and big news moments. There will be three major televised presidential debates that the whole world will be watching closely. We’ll be raising even more money to build an unrivaled Get Out the Vote operation.

There are 68 days until Election Day, Derek. And it’s an election that will determine the very identity and value system of this country. **You’ve already played a huge role on this team, and we’re going to need you at every step of the way going forward. Can you commit to a quick $1 right now -- and get us just a little closer to making history together?** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjY0MDM1ODgS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgwMjU1ODAxS0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you, Derek, for always having Hillary’s back. She’s got yours, too.

Robby

Robby Mook
Campaign Manager
Hillary for America

|
**Your donation means more today than it will later -- chip in $1 right now to help us win on November 8th:**

| [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjY0MDM1ODgS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgwMjU1ODAxS0&mt=1&rt=0)

Thanks so much for doing everything you can to elect Hillary Clinton as our next president.
Hillary for America, P.O. Box 5256, New York, NY 10185

body:
Just 1 week until the Leipsic crab feast in support of John Carney for Governor!

Get your tickets now: just $50 for all-you-can-eat
crabs plus more!

Friday, September 9, 2016
6:00-8:00 p.m.
Leipsic Volunteer Fire Company
318 Main Street
Leipsic, DE 19901

$50 per ticket

You can RSVP online by using this link: www.johncarney.org/crabs or you can contact our campaign office at 302-395-0707 or rsvp@johncarney.org.

Checks should be made payable to Friends For John Carney and mailed to:
Friends for John Carney, P.O. Box 2162, Wilmington, DE 19899

Checks can also be brought to the event.

Team Carney

Forward to a Friend: https://act.myngp.com/el/-7066177369014272000/-5612585746843041792

The Friends for John Carney Campaign may accept contributions from individuals, corporations, labor organizations, partnerships, and other entities up to $1,200 per election period. Contributions are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes.

Only individuals who are American citizens or permanent resident aliens that reside in the United States may contribute. In addition, only corporations who are incorporated in the United States, who utilize U.S. proceeds for the contribution, and for which the decision to contribute was made by an American citizen or permanent resident alien may contribute.

Contributions from corporations, companies, organizations, incorporated or unincorporated associations, general or limited partnerships, or other entities that contribute must:
- Include the full names and mailing addresses of all individuals who own a legal or equitable interest of 50% or greater in that entity, or notify us that no such person exists (the entity’s contribution is allocated pro-rata to identified persons, subject to limits);
-Include a responsible party if the aggregate amount of contributions during the election period exceeds $100; and
-Include the following form: http://elections.delaware.gov/pubs/cf_corporate_contribution_notice.pdf.

---------------------------------------------
| Paid for by Friends for John Carney |
---------------------------------------------

Friends for John Carney
19 E Commons Blvd
2nd Floor
New Castle DE 19720 United States

Peter, there’s no greater challenge than making our country great again, and you can believe me, I’m putting together an incredible team to do it -- but that team isn’t complete without you. body:
_This is our Link of the Week email -- the things that we're sharing around the office, and we think everyone on Team Maggie should know about. We hope you'll enjoy clicking around as much as we do._
Since students across the Granite State headed back to school this week, we thought it would be a great time to revisit an important vocabulary lesson:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd715/2a380d31/11adb9b0/fa0d85/3300541979/VEsH/ "http://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/endorse")
For extra credit, here's some extra reading from the Washington Post about why trying to rewrite the dictionary to explain your support for Trump is like trying to **["hug someone as you are running away from them."](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd715/2a380d31/11adb9b0/fa0d86/3300541979/VEsE/ "https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/the-fix/wp/2016/05/17/dear-republican-candidates-you-cant-have-it-both-ways-with-donald-trump/")**
-- Team Maggie
P.S. -- Here's another definition that's worth knowing:
**contribute,** _verb._ --
to give something to help a person, group, cause, or organization;
to help or cause something to happen

name: Rob Portman
subject: 3.5 million voter contacts
body:
| Peter,

We've had a terrific summer, and today I have more great news to share -- thanks to thousands of volunteers across Ohio, we've now contacted more than 3.5 million voters since May of last year!

This summer, we had over 500 full-time interns working throughout the state making calls and knocking on the doors of Ohio voters. We also launched a two-week, 50-stop "Moving Ohio Forward" Summer RV Tour that covered 30 counties and 3,500 miles with events in Cincinnati, Dayton, Toledo, Maumee, Miamisburg, Delaware, Columbus, Bergholz, Hanoverton, Tiffin, Mayfield Heights, Akron, Lima, Zanesville, Cambridge, West Chester, and many more.

[](https://www.robportman.com/watch_rv_tour_highlights?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=summer_recap&n=1)
Jane, Sally, and I had a great time meeting with supporters, volunteers, union members, students, veterans, and friends all across Ohio. We know that our team won't be outworked because this election is so important.

In this election, Ohio voters have a choice between our positive message of pro-growth policies for better wages and more jobs or Ted Strickland's disastrous policies of higher taxes, bigger government, and crushing regulations.

Thank you for your support -- onward to victory in November! [You can click here to sign up to volunteer](https://www.robportman.com/volunteer?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=summer_recap&n=2).

All best,

Rob Portman [](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=summer_recap&n=3)[](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=summer_recap&n=4)[

Friend, He did it again. Once again, Greg Gianforte was caught playing fast and loose with the truth. **Please read and help us spread the word about his long track record of just making stuff up with**[**a donation of $15.**](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=t9TOXOjo7VdHFD0E8l5Sbe19KuOHPUo9)

Here is what led the evening news last week -- **"_An_ ABC/Fox Montana _fact-checking investigation finds misleading statements made by Greg Gianforte... "_**

| We had another great week hearing from Vermonters around the state and sharing Phil’s ideas to strengthen our economy! Here are a few of this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign.

**REMEMBERING IRENE**
Five years ago this week, Tropical Storm Irene swept through Vermont and forever changed the landscape of our state. But as soon as the sky cleared rebuilding began, and neighbors came together to help each other get back on solid ground. Like Vermonters around the state, Team Scott took time on the fifth anniversary of the storm to reflect on the damage done, and our state’s recovery and resilience. **Phil visited several of the communities impacted by Irene to remember what we saw in the storm's wake, and celebrate how we are always _Vermont Strong_. **Read more about some of the communities that thrived in the face of extreme adversity [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ded3fdc78e&e=19cebfe173) and see Phil’s reflections on [Fox44](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=055cb6f6eb&e=19cebfe173).

**Photo Credit:**[**Phil Scott for Vermont Facebook**](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f6708e1cdf&e=19cebfe173)

***
**PHIL: A CANDIDATE WHO CAN BRIDGE THE DIVIDE**
It’s rare today to see a candidate that appeals to people of all political stripes. But given the challenges we face, we are seeing broad support for Phil, who has shown he is **the only candidate committed to addressing affordability in Vermont.**

The latest sign of a willingness to cross the aisle at the state level comes from Heidi Spear and Seth Henry, who told the _Valley Reporter_ this week that while they’ll stay in the D column at the national level, they expect to support Independent and Republican candidates at the state level. They cite numerous reasons, notably that **Vermont Democratic leadership has spent well beyond what Vermonters are able to afford** for ambitious programs that didn’t yield results. They write, **“In aggregate, the costs of the failure of our Vermont Democrats to effectively govern are staggering. State spending has increased by over $3,000 per year per household since Shumlin took office.”** Promising to vote for candidates that prioritize fiscal responsibility and affordability – cornerstones to Phil Scott’s platform – they continue, **“****Vermont Democratic leadership won’t solve our problems when they won’t even acknowledge they exist** – let alone their instrumental role in creating them. **Allowing affordability and economic opportunity to be undermined by delusions of grandeur, ineffective governance and gravy for vested interests is _bad for Vermont_.**”
Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=5e32918e88&e=19cebfe173). To learn more about Phil’s plans to address the exact issues Ms. Spear and Mr. Henry address in their article, click [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=16eb34e3c7&e=19cebfe173).

*******
**STAY INFORMED AT UPCOMING DEBATES**
There is a clear choice in this election. **Phil Scott is the only candidate who is focused on growing the economy, making Vermont more affordable and **[**restoring Vermonters’ faith and trust in their government**](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=452faf925a&e=19cebfe173)**.** In contrast to his opponent, he has [set strict budget limits](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=7f31c223b4&e=19cebfe173), has a plan to [modernize state government](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=aa8b89cc52&e=19cebfe173), a plan to [combat the opiate crisis](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4adc210648&e=19cebfe173), and real ideas to [address our crumbling infrastructure](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=75a6478053&e=19cebfe173).

While Phil wants to **maximize his time connecting directly with Vermonters** around the state, we appreciate that debates between all gubernatorial candidates are valuable opportunities to further highlight the differences between Phil and his opponents. The campaign is, therefore, excited to participate in ten more debates before the November 8 election (11 total throughout the General Election). Learn more about the upcoming debates [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=f23e5e8e2a&e=19cebfe173) and follow our Events page to see how you can attend public debates [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=286f31a0bb&e=19cebfe173).
***
**FARMS, FAIRS AND FRIENDS**
Our adventures this week allowed us to explore one of our state’s greatest industries, our agriculture sector. Team Scott checked out the **Rowell Farm nursery** and had **Breakfast at the Farm in Franklin County**. We also toured **Farrell Distributing** and **Burlington Electric** , met with Vermonters in **Stowe** and **Swanton** , and stopped by **UVM** and the **Bondville** and **Caledonia County** Fairs.

**Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT**
As part of our efforts to commemorate the fifth anniversary of Irene, Team Scott and our friend **Randy Brock** visited some of the hard-hit sites, attending the **Rochester Park** dedication and visiting with members of the **South Royalton** community.

**Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT**
Want to participate in an upcoming event? Check out our Events page [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=0b080b600b&e=19cebfe173).
***
**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_Phil: Priorities for a stronger future_**
“Here are the real issues in this campaign: State spending and the costs of living are growing too quickly, and our economy is growing too slowly. Over six years of one-party rule, growth in the state budget and taxes have outpaced growth in wages.” (Source: Burlington Free Press: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=c4671a58b2&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil on Irene recovery_**
"A number of things we've learned from the storm, one is that when we work together we can accomplish great things, I think we have a sense of resiliency that is part of our culture.” (Source: WCAX: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=b294d7bf60&e=19cebfe173))

**_Scott will put state on right track_**
“Phil Scott understands that making Vermont more business friendly and encouraging growth of jobs is the top priority. Until the state is prosperous again there will be increasing social issues and needs to fix.” Mary Daly, Fairlee (Source: Burlington Free Press: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=fb4619f926&e=19cebfe173))

**_Help Stop Over Regulation and Over Taxation_**
“With the very real possibility of Phil Scott as our next Governor, we have the opportunity to stop the over regulation and taxation of Vermonters.” Rep. Mark Higley (Orleans-Lamoille district) (Source: Caledonian Record: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ac947e64f8&e=19cebfe173))

**_Clean Energy, Done the Vermont Way_**
“Phil Scott believes the millions of dollars in tax subsidies being poured into renewable energy would be better spent developing renewables on a Vermont scale—residential and agricultural—not industrial.” – Dave Kelley, Greensboro (Source: Mountain Times: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=90169bd168&e=19cebfe173))
***
**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**
**_Losing the Vermont We Love...For What?_**
In an op-ed contributed to _VT Digger_ , co-directors of Grafton Woodlands Group Anna Vesely Pilette and Carol Lind made an excellent argument against industrial wind projects – a stance Phil has long-supported. As Phil has contended, the authors assert:

“Wind energy projects destroy natural resources...Furthermore, wind energy is not cost effective; it has negative economic impacts on tourism and property sales. Surrounding towns are affected with no benefit to them. It provides few, if any, permanent jobs for Vermonters. It pits neighbor against neighbor, and the permitting process is undemocratic.”

To understand more about this issue, read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=3ed85138df&e=19cebfe173) and learn more about Phil’s position on the issue [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=669d76cc75&e=19cebfe173).

**_Dealing with Vermont’s Crisis of Affordability_**
Frank Huard, a candidate for state representative for the Orleans Caledonia House district contributed a Letter to the Editor to the Caledonian Record this week, detailing his thoughts on the crisis of affordability faced by Vermonters – something both he and Phil will fight to address in Montpelier. As he states in the article, “if we as leaders of this state do not address the irresponsible spending of state government, and find a way to grow the economy, there will be no prosperity for these segments of our population that are living day to day.” He concludes, “Phil Scott will need help reeling in this outrageous approach to budgeting and will need colleagues in the Legislature passing responsible, sustainable budgets.” Phil and all Republican candidates will need your support to help address the serious challenges we face, and that starts with your vote in November! Read the full letter [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=af792933f0&e=19cebfe173).
*******
Thanks to everyone that came out to events this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9141a09bdc&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=b3a28e1606&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,
Team Scott

name: Jay Inslee
subject: You’re the best
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Thanks to you, we hit our August goal.

Dear Friend,

Thank you.

The team just gave me an update on our August fundraising. Thanks to you, we did even better than we hoped. You helped us meet -- and exceed -- our ambitious goal. You helped prove the strength of our movement when we needed it the most.

With just ten weeks left in this campaign, with 50 days until voting begins, your support provides us with the resources to compete in every corner of the state.

My opponent is counting on the deep pockets of a handful of wealthy buddies to help him out. By contrast, our campaign is powered by thousands of supporters like you. And I wouldn't have it any other way. We're not just going to win, we're going to win the right way -- by strengthening our movement from the grassroots on up.

Knowing I can count on your support means so much to me.

Now, with our opponent and his ultra-rich backers going all-in against us, will you start our September off strong and chip in $5 today?

CONTRIBUTE: http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=OTQ1NzY5NAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=2&j=MTAwMDE2NjQ5MgS2&mt=1&rt=0

It will be a tough fight over the next ten weeks. We can't afford to ease up. The GOP will use every desperate tactic they can to win. That's why your continuing support and friendship can make a difference.

Thank you, again.

Very truly yours,

Jay

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Hillary and I could use your help: here's how
body:
| Derek --

I’ve run in a lot of tough races, at every level of government. Virginia is a big, diverse, and dynamic state, and it’s been an extraordinary honor to be elected to serve as a mayor, governor, and senator.

But let me assure you: I couldn’t have pulled off any of these victories without a dedicated, passionate army of volunteers. And Hillary and I are counting on that same urgency, fiery spirit, and can-do attitude from our team in these final two months.

Have you ever thought about volunteering on a presidential campaign, Derek? **[If so, sign up to join me on an exclusive call on September 6th. I’m going to be talking about what volunteers do, why it matters, how much fun you can have -- and how much good you can do for our country. RSVP to join me on the call before space fills up.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MjY0MTA3MjkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgwMzgyNDk1S0&mt=1&rt=0)**

Volunteers are the muscle and the heart of every campaign, whether you’re vying for a seat on the Richmond City Council or waging the fight of a lifetime to serve the American people in the White House. The essential work that gets done on the ground in key states will make the difference between victory and defeat on November 8th.

I’m not going to quit fighting until we win this election. Hillary isn’t either. And we’re counting on a hardworking team of her best supporters to volunteer and stand with her in this moment in history.

**Space is limited for this call, Derek, so don’t wait to sign up. Even if you’re just curious about volunteering, join me on the phone on September 6th:**
[](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MjY0MTA3MjkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgwMzgyNDk1S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you so much,

Tim

|
**Hillary needs you now more than ever -- chip in $1 and be one of the more than 2 million grassroots donors standing with her:**

| [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjY0MTA3MjkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgwMzgyNDk1S0&mt=1&rt=0)

---

**** | | Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2 million donors committed to electing Hillary Clinton (and keeping Donald Trump out of the White House).
---
Contributions or gifts to Hillary for America are not tax deductible.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This is your campaign, so if you have thoughts on anything at all, just click [here](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=15&ms=MjY0MTA3MjkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgwMzgyNDk1S0&mt=1&rt=0) to send us a message! _**1\. Missouri (D) OPEN** : __This is**Republicans ' best pickup opportunity this year.** They nominated a**charismatic former Navy SEAL** , Eric Greitens, to try to take the seat over from term-limited Democratic Gov. It's important that you know about this new ad. | Derek --

This week, the Trump campaign spent $10 million blanketing the airwaves in battleground states with a new, incredibly dishonest ad about Hillary’s plans for our economy.

Trump thinks he can get traction on this particular issue by playing up his supposed business acumen -- so we have to push back. If we're able to outduel him over the economy, it will be that much harder for him to win.

That's one of the reasons why we feel anxious about how much he raised this past month -- if it turns out that he matched us, things are going to get tight. **[We have to be able to fight back -- will you be one of the 11 donors we need from your area today? You’ll get a free sticker as a thank you.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=MjY0MTI4MDQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODgwNDAxNTczS0&mt=1&rt=0)

** We're taking the time to tell you about this new ad not because we thought we might escape this campaign without facing any new attacks over the air -- but because we are seeing a real shift in strategy from the Trump campaign. After this, we now know what we can expect every week between today and Election Day -- more and more ads against Hillary with millions of dollars behind each one.

We need to be able to fight back while also running a fully-funded Get Out the Vote operation -- we can’t afford to compromise on either front, but if we don’t raise the resources we need, there are some tough choices we might have to make.

We had a great August, but if we don’t keep up the pace and stay with him every step of the way, we’re giving Trump a golden opportunity to erase the lead we’ve built.

The campaign is counting on you. While Trump writes himself million-dollar checks, we’re sending emails like this one. You are all that stands between him and the White House, and we have just 67 days left to win. **Can you chip in right now?**

body:
Democrats Attack Greitens Days Before Missouri GOP Primary

By Maggie Severns, POLITICO
7/28/2016 5:17 PM EST

A political group affiliated with the Democratic Governors Association is reserving ad time to attack GOP gubernatorial candidate Eric Greitens in Missouri, seeking to affect the race just five days before Republicans choose their nominee from a crowded field.

Two sources tracking media spending in the state said that Jobs and Opportunity, a 527 group funded by the DGA, has booked about a half-million dollars in broadcast TV and additional radio spending attacking Greitens. The DGA did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

The ad is set to start airing Friday. Audio, which a source said was from the ad, criticizes Greitens for accepting money from a donor accused of sexually abusing a woman.

"What does this victim accused of human trafficking have to do with your vote for governor? Well a man accused of abusing her for 13 years gave Eric Greitens $1 million," a narrator says in the ad. "While others returned his money, Greitens said he was honored to take it."

Greitens, a former Navy SEAL and political newcomer, has been picking up steam in the competitive GOP primary because of his charisma and status as an outsider. The ad is a clear indication that Democrats would rather not face Greitens in the general election. Polls have found the race between Greitens and the three other candidates extremely close, but a SurveyUSA poll released this week did find Greitens leading the pack with 25 percent, with opponent John Brunner behind him at 21 percent.

Missouri was the site of a notorious instance of Democratic primary meddling four years ago, when Democratic Sen. Claire McCaskill's campaign aired ads casting GOP Rep. Todd Akin as conservative before his Senate primary. Akin won the three-way Republican race and promptly imploded in the general election, losing to McCaskill by more than 15 percentage points.

The DGA tried to influence Republicans' gubernatorial primary in Colorado in 2014, funding a group that tried to boost conservative ex-Rep. Tom Tancredo. But the effort prompted a backlash and Tancredo ultimately lost the GOP primary.

The winner in the Missouri GOP primary will face current Attorney General Chris Koster, a moderate and rising star in the Democratic Party, in the general election.
